extra.reasoning: The variable `pallor` does not appear in the condition of any rule (r1 fires on sweating, r2 on severe_headache, r3 on fever) nor in the default. Therefore acuity is entirely independent of pallor. For any fixed assignment of sweating/fever/severe_headache, flipping pallor from false to true leaves the matching rule set unchanged, so the computed acuity is identical: it is `urgent` whenever any of sweating/fever/severe_headache is true (all three matching rules set acuity := urgent at the same priority, so ties never matter), and otherwise the default makes it `routine`. Since acuity_after always equals acuity_before, it is never strictly lower, satisfying the monotonicity property (equality is permitted by "no lower than before"). No counterexample exists; the property holds.
